flags.name: index
flags.desc: Insert position; appended to the end when omitted

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm high-risk write (exit code 10 without this flag)

flags.name: source-index
flags.desc: Source position (0-based); optional. If omitted, the CLI runtime derives it from the current workbook index of `--sheet-id` / `--sheet-name`

flags.name: title
flags.desc: Copy title; auto-generated by the server when omitted

flags.name: index
flags.desc: Insert position for the copy (0-based); appended to the end when omitted

flags.name: color
flags.desc: Hex color like `#FF0000`; pass empty string `""` to clear

flags.name: folder-token
flags.desc: Target folder token; placed at the drive root when omitted

flags.name: headers
flags.desc: Header row as a JSON array: `["Col A","Col B"]`

flags.name: values
flags.desc: Initial data as a 2D JSON array: `[["alice",95]]`

flags.name: sheet-id
flags.desc: Required only in csv mode: which sheet to export as CSV. This is a `+workbook-export`-specific flag, unrelated to the common four-tuple sheet locator (this shortcut does not accept the common sheet locator)

flags.name: output-path
flags.desc: Local save path; export is triggered but not downloaded when omitted

flags.name: include
flags.desc: Comma-separated structure info categories to return

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Limit structure info to this A1 range; whole sheet when omitted

flags.name: inherit-style
flags.desc: Style inheritance for the new row/column: `before` (from preceding) / `after` (from following) / `none` (default)

flags.name: position
flags.desc: Insert position (1-based row number like `3` or column letter like `C`); new rows/columns are inserted *before* this position

flags.name: count
flags.desc: Number of rows/columns to insert (must be > 0)

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Row/column closed range to delete; rows use 1-based numbers like `3:7` or `5` (single row), columns use letters like `C:F` or `C`

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); row/column deletion is irreversible

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Row/column closed range to hide; rows use 1-based numbers like `3:7`, columns use letters like `C:F`

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Row/column closed range to unhide; rows use 1-based numbers like `3:7`, columns use letters like `C:F`

flags.name: count
flags.desc: Freeze the first N rows/columns; pass 0 to unfreeze

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Row/column closed range to group; rows use 1-based numbers like `3:7`, columns use letters like `C:F`

flags.name: depth
flags.desc: Group nesting level to ungroup; default 1 (outermost)

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Row/column closed range to ungroup; rows use 1-based numbers like `3:7`, columns use letters like `C:F`

flags.name: source-range
flags.desc: Source row/column closed range to move; rows use 1-based numbers like `3:7`, columns use letters like `C:F`

flags.name: target
flags.desc: Destination position (the moved rows/columns are placed *before* this position); rows use 1-based row number like `12`, columns use column letter like `H`. Must match the dimension of --source-range

flags.name: range
flags.desc: A1 range, e.g. `A1:F10` (no sheet prefix — use `--sheet-id` / `--sheet-name` to select the sheet)

flags.name: skip-hidden
flags.desc: Skip hidden rows and columns; default `false`

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Target range in A1 notation, e.g. `A2:A100` (no sheet prefix — use `--sheet-id` / `--sheet-name` to select the sheet)

flags.name: range
flags.desc: A1 range, e.g. `A1:F30` (no sheet prefix — use `--sheet-id` / `--sheet-name` to select the sheet)

flags.name: include-row-prefix
flags.desc: Whether to prefix each row with `[row=N]`; default `true`

flags.name: skip-hidden
flags.desc: Skip hidden rows and columns; default `false`

flags.name: rows-json
flags.desc: Return structured rows ({row_number, values:{col→cell}}) instead of CSV text; default false

flags.name: dry-run
flags.desc: Print the request path and parameters without executing

flags.name: find
flags.desc: Text to find (interpreted as regex when `--regex` is set)

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Search range (A1 notation); whole sheet when omitted

flags.name: offset
flags.desc: Skip the first N matches (for pagination); default 0

flags.name: replacement
flags.desc: Replacement text; pass empty string `""` to delete matched content

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Replace range (A1 notation); whole sheet when omitted

flags.name: dry-run
flags.desc: Required preflight: outputs `would_replace_count` for user confirmation before the actual replace

flags.name: cells
flags.desc: JSON 2D array `[[{cell},...],...]`, dimensions must match `--range`; each cell may carry `value` / `formula` / `cell_styles` / `note` / `rich_text` (incl. `type="embed-image"` in-cell image); run `--print-schema` for full fields

flags.name: allow-overwrite
flags.desc: Allow overwriting non-empty cells (default true); set false to error if any target cell is non-empty

flags.name: copy-to-range
flags.desc: Copy-to range (A1 notation): replicate what --cells wrote into --range (values/formulas/styles, per the fields actually passed) to this range; formula refs auto-shift (C2=B2 -> C3=B3). Write a one-row/one-block template then fill a whole column/area. Supports full rows '3:6', full columns 'C:E', to-col-end 'D3:D', to-row-end 'D3:3', and comma-separated multiple targets like 'C1:D2,E5:F6'.

flags.name: number-format
flags.desc: Number format pattern (e.g. text `@`, number `0.00`, currency `$#,##0.00`, date `mm/dd/yyyy`)

flags.name: border-styles
flags.desc: Border config JSON: `{ top: {style,color,weight}, bottom: ..., left: ..., right: ... }`; same shape for all 4 sides

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Target cell (A1 notation; must be a single cell, e.g. `A1`; start and end must be identical)

flags.name: image
flags.desc: Local image path (PNG / JPEG / JPG / GIF / BMP / JFIF / EXIF / TIFF / BPG / HEIC)

flags.name: name
flags.desc: Image file name (with extension); defaults to the basename of `--image`

flags.name: options
flags.desc: Options as a JSON array, e.g. `["opt1","opt2"]`. Server enforces no item-count cap and no per-item length cap; values containing commas are accepted (they are escape-encoded on the wire). For very large lists prefer `--source-range`.

flags.name: colors
flags.desc: Per-option pill colors, RGB hex array (e.g. `["#1FB6C1","#F006C2"]`). Length may be shorter than the source (`--options` items / `--source-range` cells) — extras cycle through a 10-color palette — but never longer (CLI Validate rejects: `--colors length (N) must not exceed dropdown source size (M)`). **Applies on its own**; ignored when `--highlight=false`.

flags.name: highlight
flags.desc: Pill-highlight switch. **Omitted = ON** (options cycle through a 10-color palette). Pass `--highlight=false` for a plain dropdown. Override colors via `--colors`.

flags.name: source-range
flags.desc: Source range for listFromRange dropdown (A1 + sheet prefix, e.g. `'Sheet1'!T1:T3`); maps to server `data_validation.range` and auto-sets `data_validation.type='listFromRange'`. XOR with `--options`: pass `--options` for an inline list (type=list), pass this for a range reference (type=listFromRange). `--colors` length rule unchanged (≤ source range cell count); `--highlight` / `--multiple` behave the same. When `--highlight` is on and the source covers more than 2000 cells, the server flags the dropdown as option-error (highlight + large source is an unsupported combo); CLI emits a stderr warning. Pass `--highlight=false` to suppress.

flags.name: start-cell
flags.desc: Top-left A1 anchor (e.g. `A1`, `B5`; no sheet prefix — use `--sheet-id` / `--sheet-name` to select the sheet); must be a single cell, range notation not accepted; the bottom-right is inferred from CSV row/column counts

flags.name: csv
flags.desc: RFC 4180 CSV text; plain values only (no formulas / styles / comments)

flags.name: allow-overwrite
flags.desc: Allow overwriting (default true); set false to error if any target cell is non-empty

flags.name: range
flags.desc: alias for --start-cell (parity with +csv-get / +cells-set, which locate with --range); a range like A1:H17 collapses to its top-left cell

flags.name: scope
flags.desc: Clear scope: `content` (default, values only) / `formats` (formats only) / `all` (values and formats)

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); clear is irreversible

flags.name: type
flags.desc: Sizing mode: `pixel` (explicit px value, requires `--size`) / `standard` (reset to default row height) / `auto` (fit content)

flags.name: size
flags.desc: Row height in pixels (e.g. 30 / 40 / 60); required when `--type pixel`, ignored otherwise

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Row closed range to resize; 1-based row numbers like `2:10` or `5` (single row)

flags.name: type
flags.desc: Sizing mode: `pixel` (explicit px value, requires `--size`) / `standard` (reset to default column width)

flags.name: size
flags.desc: Column width in pixels (e.g. 80 / 120 / 200); required when `--type pixel`, ignored otherwise

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Column closed range to resize; column letters like `A:E` or `C` (single column)

flags.name: target-sheet-id
flags.desc: Destination sub-sheet id; defaults to the same sheet as the source

flags.name: target-range
flags.desc: Destination A1 range (anchor cell is enough; size inferred from the source)

flags.name: target-sheet-id
flags.desc: Destination sub-sheet id; defaults to the same sheet as the source

flags.name: target-range
flags.desc: Destination A1 range (anchor cell is enough; size inferred from the source)

flags.name: source-range
flags.desc: Fill template range (seed cells for the series)

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Sort range (A1 notation; whether the header is included depends on `--has-header`)

flags.name: has-header
flags.desc: Treat the first row as a header and exclude from sort; default `false`

flags.name: url
flags.desc: Spreadsheet locator (independent from per-operation sheet locator)

flags.name: spreadsheet-token
flags.desc: Spreadsheet locator (independent from per-operation sheet locator)

flags.name: operations
flags.desc: JSON array: [{"shortcut":"+xxx-yyy","input":{...}}, ...]. shortcut uses CLI names; input is that shortcut's flag set — it includes the per-operation sheet locator (sheet_id or sheet_name) but not the spreadsheet token/url (pass that once at the top level via --url/--spreadsheet-token; +batch-update has no top-level --sheet-id). input keys are the shortcut's flags flattened into JSON (e.g. "range":"A11:B12"), not another nested layer. For basic flags use lark-cli sheets <shortcut> --help; for composite JSON flags use --print-schema --flag-name <flag>. Do not pass an explicit operation field. Strict transaction by default, pass --continue-on-error for soft batch; no nesting; executed serially.

flags.name: continue-on-error
flags.desc: Continue with remaining operations when a sub-operation fails; default false (abort on first failure)

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm high-risk write (exit code 10 without this flag)

flags.name: dry-run
flags.desc: Print the request template for each sub-operation; no network side effects

flags.name: ranges
flags.desc: Target ranges as a JSON array (e.g. `["'Sheet1'!A1:B2","'Sheet2'!D1:D10"]`); each item must include a sheet prefix; the prefix must be the sheet display name (e.g. `Sheet1`), not the sheet reference_id; ranges may target different sheets; the same style is applied to every range

flags.name: number-format
flags.desc: Number format pattern (e.g. text `@`, number `0.00`, currency `$#,##0.00`, date `mm/dd/yyyy`)

flags.name: border-styles
flags.desc: Border config JSON (same shape as in +cells-set-style)

flags.name: ranges
flags.desc: Target ranges as a JSON array (e.g. `["'Sheet1'!A2:A100","'Sheet1'!C2:C100"]`); each item must include a sheet prefix; the prefix must be the sheet display name (e.g. `Sheet1`), not the sheet reference_id

flags.name: options
flags.desc: Options as a JSON array, e.g. `["opt1","opt2"]`. Server enforces no item-count cap and no per-item length cap; values containing commas are accepted (they are escape-encoded on the wire). For very large lists prefer `--source-range`.

flags.name: colors
flags.desc: Per-option pill colors, RGB hex array (e.g. `["#1FB6C1","#F006C2"]`). Length may be shorter than the source (`--options` items / `--source-range` cells) — extras cycle through a 10-color palette — but never longer (CLI Validate rejects: `--colors length (N) must not exceed dropdown source size (M)`). **Applies on its own**; ignored when `--highlight=false`.

flags.name: highlight
flags.desc: Pill-highlight switch. **Omitted = ON** (options cycle through a 10-color palette). Pass `--highlight=false` for a plain dropdown. Override colors via `--colors`.

flags.name: source-range
flags.desc: Source range for listFromRange dropdown (A1 + sheet prefix, e.g. `'Sheet1'!T1:T3`); maps to server `data_validation.range` and auto-sets `data_validation.type='listFromRange'`. XOR with `--options`: pass `--options` for an inline list (type=list), pass this for a range reference (type=listFromRange). `--colors` length rule unchanged (≤ source range cell count); `--highlight` / `--multiple` behave the same. When `--highlight` is on and the source covers more than 2000 cells, the server flags the dropdown as option-error (highlight + large source is an unsupported combo); CLI emits a stderr warning. Pass `--highlight=false` to suppress.

flags.name: ranges
flags.desc: Target ranges as a JSON array (up to 100 items, e.g. `["'Sheet1'!E2:E6"]`); each item must include a sheet prefix; the prefix must be the sheet display name (e.g. `Sheet1`), not the sheet reference_id

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm high-risk write (exit code 10 without this flag)

flags.name: ranges
flags.desc: Target ranges as a JSON array (e.g. `["'Sheet1'!A2:Z1000","'Sheet2'!A2:Z1000"]`); each item must include a sheet prefix; the prefix must be the sheet display name (e.g. `Sheet1`), not the sheet reference_id; ranges may target different sheets; the same scope is cleared from every range

flags.name: scope
flags.desc: Clear scope: `content` (default, values only) / `formats` (formats only) / `all` (values and formats)

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); batch clear is irreversible

flags.name: chart-id
flags.desc: Filter to a single chart reference_id

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Full chart config JSON. Top-level keys: `position` / `offset` / `size` / `snapshot` (no top-level `data`, no extra nested `properties`); chart data config lives under `snapshot.data` (`refs` / `headerMode` / `dim1` / `dim2`). Deeply nested — run `--print-schema --flag-name properties` for the full structure.

flags.name: dry-run
flags.desc: Print the request template; no side effects

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Full or sufficiently complete chart config JSON (read back with `+chart-list` first, then patch)

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag)

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: JSON: {"rows":[...],"columns":[...],"values":[...],"filters":[...],"show_row_grand_total":true,"show_col_grand_total":true} (data source goes through --source; do not put source here)

flags.name: target-position
flags.desc: Top-left cell within the target sub-sheet (A1 notation, e.g. `A1`); maps to the top-level `target_position`, default `A1` (not sent when the value is A1). It and `--range` both express placement but map to different wire fields — avoid passing conflicting values for both.

flags.name: target-sheet-id
flags.desc: Reference_id of the target sub-sheet where the pivot table will be placed (mutually exclusive with `--target-sheet-name`; takes priority when both given; when both omitted, a new sub-sheet is auto-created to host the pivot — recommended). Distinct from the data-source sheet, which lives inside --source as a 'Sheet'-prefixed A1 reference like 'Sheet1'!A1:D100.

flags.name: target-sheet-name
flags.desc: Name of the target sub-sheet where the pivot table will be placed (mutually exclusive with `--target-sheet-id`; when both omitted, a new sub-sheet is auto-created to host the pivot — recommended). Distinct from the data-source sheet, which lives inside --source as a 'Sheet'-prefixed A1 reference like 'Sheet1'!A1:D100.

flags.name: source
flags.desc: Pivot table source range (A1 notation; format `'SheetName'!StartCell:EndCell`, e.g. `'Sheet1'!A1:D100`)

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Pivot table top-left placement (single A1 value, e.g. `F1`; create only), maps to `properties.range`; placed at the top-left of the target sub-sheet (a newly created one by default) when omitted. It and `--target-position` both express placement but map to different wire fields — avoid passing conflicting values for both.

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Full or sufficiently complete pivot config (read back with `+pivot-list --pivot-table-id <id>` first, then patch)

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); delete is irreversible

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Rule config JSON: `style` (required, applied on match), `attrs?` (rule-type-dependent params), `has_ref?`. `rule_type` and `ranges` are separate flags

flags.name: rule-type
flags.desc: Conditional format rule type; takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`

flags.name: ranges
flags.desc: A1 ranges where the conditional format applies, as a JSON array (e.g. `["A1:A100","C2:C50"]`); takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Rule config JSON, same shape as `+cond-format-create --properties`; update overwrites the entire rule

flags.name: rule-type
flags.desc: Conditional format rule type; takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`

flags.name: ranges
flags.desc: A1 ranges where the conditional format applies, as a JSON array (e.g. `["A1:A100","C2:C50"]`); takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); delete is irreversible

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Filter range (A1 notation, including header row, e.g. `A1:F1000`); do not duplicate the range field inside `--properties`

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Filter rule JSON: `rules` (per-column rule array), `filtered_columns?` (active column index hint). The flag is optional overall — if provided, `rules` must be non-empty; if omitted, an empty filter is created on `--range` (no column conditions). `range` is a separate flag (do not duplicate inside this JSON)

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Filter rule JSON: `rules` and `filtered_columns?`; update overwrites the entire rule set (pass `rules: []` to clear). `range` is a separate flag

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Range the filter applies to (A1 notation, e.g. `A1:F1000`); takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); delete is irreversible

flags.name: view-id
flags.desc: Filter by filter-view reference_id (returns the matching single view)

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Filter-view rule JSON: `rules?` (per-column rule array), `filtered_columns?`. `range` and `view_name` are separate flags

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Range the filter view applies to (A1 notation, e.g. `A1:F1000`); takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`; required on create and must cover the header row

flags.name: view-name
flags.desc: Filter-view name; auto-assigned by the server when omitted on create, kept unchanged when omitted on update; takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: Filter-view rule JSON: `rules?`, `filtered_columns?`; update overwrites the entire rule set (read back with `+filter-view-list` first, then patch; pass `rules: []` to clear). `range` and `view_name` are separate flags

flags.name: range
flags.desc: Range the filter view applies to (A1 notation, e.g. `A1:F1000`); takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`; omit to keep the current range on update

flags.name: view-name
flags.desc: Filter-view name; auto-assigned by the server when omitted on create, kept unchanged when omitted on update; takes precedence over the same-named field inside `--properties`

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm high-risk write (exit code 10 without this flag)

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: JSON: `{config (shared style), sparklines (array of mini-charts)}`; run `--print-schema` for the full structure

flags.name: properties
flags.desc: JSON: `{config, sparklines}`; read back with `+sparkline-list --group-id <id>` first, then patch; run `--print-schema` for the full structure

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); delete is irreversible

flags.name: float-image-id
flags.desc: Filter by id; lists all float images on the sheet when omitted

flags.name: image-token
flags.desc: Image file_token (XOR with `--image-uri`). Common source: `image_token` returned by `+float-image-list`

flags.name: image-uri
flags.desc: Image reference_id (XOR with `--image-token`); the reference_id returned by the image upload flow

flags.name: position-row
flags.desc: Row anchor of the image's top-left corner (0-based)

flags.name: position-col
flags.desc: Column anchor of the image's top-left corner (column letter, e.g. `A` / `B`)

flags.name: offset-row
flags.desc: Pixel offset within the anchor row, on top of `--position-row`

flags.name: offset-col
flags.desc: Pixel offset within the anchor column, on top of `--position-col`

flags.name: image
flags.desc: Local image path; the CLI uploads it as a sheet_image and uses the returned file_token (XOR with --image-token / --image-uri)

flags.name: image-token
flags.desc: Image file_token (XOR with `--image-uri`). Common source: `image_token` returned by `+float-image-list`

flags.name: image-uri
flags.desc: Image reference_id (XOR with `--image-token`); the reference_id returned by the image upload flow

flags.name: position-row
flags.desc: Row anchor of the image's top-left corner (0-based)

flags.name: position-col
flags.desc: Column anchor of the image's top-left corner (column letter, e.g. `A` / `B`)

flags.name: offset-row
flags.desc: Pixel offset within the anchor row, on top of `--position-row`

flags.name: offset-col
flags.desc: Pixel offset within the anchor column, on top of `--position-col`

flags.name: yes
flags.desc: Confirm destructive write (exit code 10 without this flag); delete is irreversible
